channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Facing competition from Big Tech, states dangle incentives and loosen laws to attract power plants
item.description: Facing spiking energy demand, U.S. states are pressing for ways to build new power plants faster as policymakers try to protect residents from higher electric bills and keep their economies on track.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Dakota law raises questions about future of massive Midwest pipeline
item.description: SIOUX FALLS, S.D. (AP) — A new law in South Dakota prohibiting the use of eminent domain to acquire land for carbon capture pipelines raises questions about the viability of a proposed 2,500 mile (4,023-kilometer) project snaking through five Midwest states.
item.dc:creator: SARAH RAZA and JACK DURA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress unveils spending plan after Trump calls on Republicans to avoid government shutdown
item.description: Republicans are unveiling a new plan to fund the government past the coming shutdown deadline next week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump has created a task force to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe's premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when his on-again, off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP campaign chairman says voters will 'reward us' for the Trump-Musk DOGE cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Almost two months into President Donald Trump's second term, the chairman of the House Republican campaign committee is already predicting his party will pick up seats in the midterm elections some 20 months away. Rep. Richard Hudson, R-N.C.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. DeSantis makes push to repeal Florida's red flag laws
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis signaled that he wants to repeal the state's red flag law, which was approved after the Feb. 2018 mass shooting in Parkland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump order denies student loan relief to nonprofit workers engaged in 'improper' activity
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is ordering changes to the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program that would disqualify workers of nonprofit groups deemed to have engaged in “improper” activities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Democrats ask Rubio for answers on now-canceled $400 million Tesla plan
item.description: A letter from two House Democrats presses Rubio for details about who approved an effort to try to use hundreds of millions of dollars of taxpayer money on armored electric vehicles from Tesla.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump admin investigation finds Maine Dept. of Education violated Title IX
item.description: On Feb. 25, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services sent a notice of violation against the Maine Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: 'Final Moments' With Iran
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics conten
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Housing policies cited as major reason families forced to give up pets
item.description: Pet rescues and animal shelters hear it over and over: a family has to give up their dog because they had to move and the new place has a rule.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about Social Security office closures driven by Musk's DOGE
item.description: Across-the-board cuts at the Social Security Administration are prompting questions about how the benefits of millions of recipients may be affected. Among the potential changes are layoffs for more than 10% of the agency’s workforce and the closure of dozens of offices throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk tells Republican lawmakers he's not to blame for federal firings
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s not his fault. Billionaire Elon Musk is telling Republican lawmakers that he is not to blame for the firings of thousands of federal workers, including veterans, as pushes to downsize the government.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans press Trump to resume military, intelligence aid to Ukraine
item.description: Republican lawmakers are starting to urge President Trump to reverse his decisions to pause U.S. military and intelligence assistance to Ukraine, warning that a prolonged stoppage of U.S. help for the war effort would have a seriously detrimental effect.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: New fines and awareness help reduce litter on Minnesota lakes, but advocates want more action
item.description: Lake advocates say a recent state law is helping curb the problem of trash left on Minnesota’s frozen lakes. But they're hoping for more action, including passage of a bill requiring state agencies establish a statewide “Keep It Clean” program and provide funding for local cleanup efforts.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Dozens of conservative leaders back clean stopgap to avert shutdown
item.description: Dozens of major conservative activist leaders and organizers are urging conservatives in Congress to support a clean continuing resolution (CR) to avert a government shutdown in a memo organized by the Conservative Action Project.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Minnesota's special state House election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Voters in Minnesota will choose a new representative Tuesday in a heavily Democratic state House district at the center of a post-Election Day drama to control the chamber. The special election in House District 40B, in the northern St.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Education Dept. shutdown 'needs to be priority': Jonathan Butcher
item.description: "The big picture here is that not all of it is essential. … So much of what the department does is not essential," Jonathan Butcher says.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Aces could join Las Vegas teams with their own specialty license plates
item.description: Las Vegas Aces fans might get a chance to show their support as the state considers issuing a specialty license plate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tim Scott unveils bill targeting debanking
item.description: Debanking — the closure of accounts that banks consider risky, often with little notice or explanation — has become a key issue for Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Trump stresses Cabinet secretaries lead agency cuts, but keeps Musk as threat
item.description: President Trump on Thursday stressed that his Cabinet secretaries take the lead on making cuts to their agencies and staff, insisting that officials are empowered to make decisions before Elon Musk can step in. “I want the Cabinet members, go first, keep all the people you want.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Tensions flare in House over Al Green censure
item.description: Tensions flared in the House on Thursday as lawmakers voted to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his disruptive protest during President Trump’s address to Congress, prompting a dramatic scene on the House floor — and demands from Republicans for further retribution.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: Trump was slated to sign an order calling for the dismantling of the department Thursday, but the White House now says it will be signed in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Use of military aircraft for deportations has stopped, source says
item.description: A Defense Department source says the Pentagon has not received a request for military aircraft since Saturday, March 1.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Democrats react to Trump's speech; state Republicans applaud his vision
item.description: Democrats who represent Nevada in Washington, D.C., condemned what they see as a failure by President Donald Trump to lower prices for Nevada families, while state Republicans applauded his leadership the morning after his address to a joint session of Congress Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: LA Times to give op-eds AI-generated political rating, list of alternate views
item.description: The Los Angeles Times has launched an AI-driven "bias meter" to provide readers with alternative perspectives on opinion content.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: DOGE cuts all GSA employees across Nevada, senator says
item.description: President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) fired all General Services Administration employees across Nevada on Monday, according to Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: The Education Department sent out an email to staffers on Friday saying if they quit by Monday at midnight, they will receive a $25,000 cash payout.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, Fox 5

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC
